name: RBAC Permission Enumeration Attempt
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Leo Tsaousis (@laripping)
SIGMA Title: RBAC Permission Enumeration Attempt
SIGMA Description: Detects identities attempting to enumerate their Kubernetes RBAC permissions.
In the early stages of a breach, attackers will aim to list the permissions they have within the compromised environment.
In a Kubernetes cluster, this can be achieved by interacting with the API server, and querying the SelfSubjectAccessReview API via e.g. a "kubectl auth can-i --list" command.
This will enumerate the Role-Based Access Controls (RBAC) rules defining the compromised user's authorization.


False positives:
- Unknown

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%create%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%authorization.k8s.io%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%selfsubjectrulesreviews%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY RBAC Permission Enumeration Attempt on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event matches LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%create%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%authorization.k8s.io%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%selfsubjectrulesreviews%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: RBAC Permission Enumeration Attempt
